mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive forward returns exceed 55% when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances when UNRATE crosses the specified threshold over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive forward returns in greater than 50% of cases over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates into immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative forward returns in greater than 65% of observations during recovery phases over a 15-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent payroll growth and declining unemployment confirm a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 55% of triggered months over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects expanding economic output and corporate profit potential, which systematically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all 3-year rolling windows or positive returns occur in less than 45% of high-GDP quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with restrictive policy rates compresses real discount rates and corporate margins, forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of observations when both thresholds are breached over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and improving supply-side efficiency, which systematically produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rising INDPRO fails to generate positive forward returns in greater than 50% of cases during early-cycle phases over a 15-year sample.
